action.reasoning: Observation: 30-epoch ConvNeXt got best val 0.841 at epoch 10, then DRW kicked in at epoch 18 and HURT performance (dropped to 0.78-0.83). The best model was actually pre-DRW. My current best.pt was overwritten with this run's epoch 10 (val 0.841) — actually need to check what was saved. The previous test eval was 0.8351 with the 20-epoch run. Analysis: DRW with strong class weights is hurting. Logit adjustment alone (already in loss) is doing the heavy lifting. Pre-DRW model at epoch 10 hit 0.841 val — promising. Decision: Drop DRW (or weaken it), train ~20 epochs without DRW, just use logit adjustment + mixup/cutmix. Also try CLIP ViT-B/16 with better hyperparameters separately. First check what's in checkpoints/best.pt.
action.command: python -c "import torch; c=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('arch:', c.get('arch')); print('val_bal:', c.get('val_bal_acc')); print('epoch:', c.get('epoch'))"
